title: PM Modi Holds High-Level Meeting with Union Secretaries on Self-Reliance, Ease of Doing Business, AI and Cybersecurity
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a high-level meeting with Union Secretaries on June 30, 2026, focusing on two key themes: ease of doing business/ease of living, and self-reliance (Atmanirbharta). Discussions also covered cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and reducing dependence on Chinese imports. Modi urged secretaries to break inter-ministerial silos and push for convergence across departments to accelerate policy delivery.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 2 (Governance) — questions on executive coordination, policy implementation, and the role of the civil services/bureaucracy in governance reform. Also connects to Atmanirbhar Bharat as a policy theme.
key_facts: Meeting chaired by PM Modi with Union Secretaries on June 30, 2026
Key themes: Ease of Doing Business, Ease of Living, Self-Reliance (Atmanirbharta)
Discussions included AI, cybersecurity, and reducing Chinese import dependence
PM stressed 'breaking silos' and inter-departmental coordination
source: NDTV / The Hindu / Times of India

title: Amit Shah Launches New FCRA Portal and e-OCI Card
summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah launched a revamped Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA) portal and the e-OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card on June 30, 2026. The new FCRA portal is aimed at improving transparency and digital compliance for NGOs and organisations receiving foreign funding. The e-OCI card is a digital version of the OCI document, aimed at easing services for the Indian diaspora. The Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026 has also been under constitutional scrutiny.
exam_angle: FCRA is a frequently tested topic in GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) — its provisions, 2020 amendments, and implications for civil society. OCI status and its rights/limitations is also an exam favourite under Indian citizenship provisions.
key_facts: FCRA: Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act governs foreign funding to NGOs/associations
New FCRA portal launched for digital compliance and transparency
e-OCI card: digital Overseas Citizen of India card launched for diaspora
Launched by: Union Home Minister Amit Shah

title: Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of Electoral Rolls in Karnataka: Controversy Over Voting Rights and Government Benefits
summary: The Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Karnataka has triggered a political row, with Chief Minister Siddaramaiah stating that citizens without voting rights may risk losing access to government welfare benefits. The BJP alleged that the Karnataka government is stalling SIR by not appointing Booth Level Officers (BLOs). The Congress party called the Election Commission of India (ECI) a 'puppet' and demanded changes to the SIR exercise. SIR is a process for comprehensive revision of voter lists by the ECI.
exam_angle: Connects to GS Paper 2 (Polity) — Electoral Roll revision processes, role of ECI under Article 324, voter registration, and linkage of electoral participation with welfare entitlements raises constitutional concerns regarding right to equality and right to vote.
key_facts: SIR: Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls conducted by Election Commission of India
BLOs (Booth Level Officers) are key functionaries in voter roll revision
ECI derives authority from Article 324 of the Constitution
Controversy: linking government benefits to voting rights raises rights-based concerns

title: Ethanol Blending (E20) Programme: Government Clarifies 'Experiment' Remark Before Supreme Court
summary: The Indian government told the Supreme Court that the E20 ethanol blending programme (blending 20% ethanol in petrol) is being evaluated for results expected by next year, amid concerns about its impact on vehicle engines and food security. The government later clarified it did not call E20 an 'experiment' before the Supreme Court. E20 is a key component of India's National Biofuel Policy aimed at reducing crude oil imports and cutting carbon emissions.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy/Environment) — ethanol blending policy, National Biofuel Policy 2018, energy security, and the Supreme Court's role in reviewing executive policies. E20 target of 20% ethanol blending by 2025-26 is an important policy milestone.
key_facts: E20: 20% ethanol blending in petrol — India's target under National Biofuel Policy
Programme is under judicial scrutiny in the Supreme Court
Ethanol blending reduces crude oil import dependence and greenhouse gas emissions
National Biofuel Policy 2018 revised target to achieve E20 by 2025-26

title: US-India Bilateral Trade Deal in 'Final Steps', Says US Ambassador Sergio Gor
summary: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor stated on June 30, 2026 that the US-India bilateral trade deal is in its 'final steps', with only about 1% of negotiations remaining. The deal is expected to significantly reshape trade relations between the two countries, covering tariffs, market access, and digital trade. The announcement comes amid broader US tariff actions globally under the Trump administration.
exam_angle: Key GS Paper 2 (IR) topic — India-US trade relations, significance of a bilateral trade agreement, and its implications for India's export competitiveness, Make in India, and Atmanirbhar Bharat.
key_facts: US Ambassador to India: Sergio Gor
US-India trade deal described as '99% done' / in 'final steps'
Deal expected to cover tariffs, market access, and digital trade
Context: broader US tariff actions under Trump administration's trade policy

title: India Slams Pakistan's Airstrikes in Afghanistan as 'Blatant Act of Aggression'
summary: India strongly condemned Pakistan's airstrikes in Afghanistan, calling them a 'blatant act of aggression'. The strikes, reportedly targeting militant positions, have caused civilian casualties and heightened regional tensions. Russia separately urged Pakistan and Afghanistan to resolve disputes through diplomacy. India's condemnation underscores its strategic interest in Afghan stability and its broader position on cross-border military actions.
exam_angle: Critical GS Paper 2 (IR) issue — India's Afghanistan policy, India-Pakistan relations, Pakistan's role in Afghan instability, and India's diplomatic stance on sovereignty and territorial integrity.
key_facts: India described Pakistan's airstrikes in Afghanistan as 'blatant act of aggression'
Russia urged both Pakistan and Afghanistan to use diplomacy
India has historically supported Afghan sovereignty and reconstruction
Strikes heighten concerns about regional stability in South/Central Asia

title: PM Modi Speaks with Iranian President Pezeshkian on West Asia Tensions; Emphasises Freedom of Navigation in Strait of Hormuz
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on June 30, 2026, ahead of the state burial of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. India stressed the need for lasting peace and stability in West Asia and emphasised the importance of freedom of navigation, particularly in the Strait of Hormuz, which is critical for India-bound energy shipments. India-bound ships were reported to have 'gone dark' in the Hormuz region amid tensions.
exam_angle: GS Paper 2 (IR) — India-Iran relations, India's energy security dependence on West Asian oil, freedom of navigation as a diplomatic principle, and India's policy of engaging all parties in regional conflicts.
key_facts: PM Modi spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on June 30, 2026
Call made ahead of state burial of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
India emphasised freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz
Strait of Hormuz: critical chokepoint for ~20% of global oil trade; vital for India's energy imports

title: Iran Warns of Closure of Strait of Hormuz; US-Iran Talks in Qatar Amid Tensions
summary: Iran warned against foreign intervention in the Strait of Hormuz, stating it would 'only complicate issues', while also signalling readiness for war if diplomatic talks fail. The US and Iran were engaged in indirect talks in Qatar on a potential ceasefire/nuclear deal, though Tehran denied imminent direct negotiations. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital global energy chokepoint through which approximately 20% of the world's oil passes.
exam_angle: GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy Security) — the Strait of Hormuz is a geopolitical hotspot with direct implications for India's oil imports, energy security, and West Asia policy.
key_facts: Strait of Hormuz: narrow waterway between Iran and Oman; ~20% of global oil passes through it
Iran warned against foreign intervention, asserted sovereign control
US-Iran indirect talks held in Qatar on ceasefire/nuclear deal
India-bound ships reported 'going dark' in the Hormuz region
source: Times of India / Reuters / The Hindu

title: IMD Forecasts Below-Normal Rainfall in July 2026; El Niño to Impact Monsoon
summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast below-normal rainfall for July 2026, with the cumulative rainfall deficit already at 40%. El Niño conditions are expected to tighten their grip on the monsoon, potentially affecting Kharif crop sowing and water reservoir levels. The Centre has prepared a contingency plan for 12 states identified as being at risk from the El Niño impact.
exam_angle: GS Paper 1 (Geography) and GS Paper 3 (Agriculture/Disaster Management) — El Niño's impact on the Indian monsoon is a recurring exam topic. Below-normal rainfall has cascading effects on agriculture, food security, and rural economy.
key_facts: IMD forecast: below-normal rainfall for July 2026
Cumulative monsoon rainfall deficit: 40% as of June 30, 2026
El Niño: warming of central/eastern Pacific Ocean that weakens Indian monsoon
Centre prepared contingency plans for 12 El Niño-at-risk states

title: Europe Heatwave 2026: Record Temperatures, 300+ Excess Deaths in France; WMO Issues Alert
summary: A record-breaking heatwave swept across Europe in June 2026, with roads melting and tram tracks buckling in Italy, France, and the Balkans. France recorded over 300 excess deaths during the May-June heatwave period, with funeral homes overwhelmed. The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) issued alerts as temperatures hit record highs across the continent. The event is being linked to climate change and the intensification of extreme weather events.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Environment/Disaster Management) — extreme heat events, urban heat island effect, WMO's role, and the connection between global warming and increasing frequency/intensity of heatwaves. Relevant to climate change discussion in GS Paper 3.
key_facts: 2026 European heatwave: record temperatures in France, Italy, Balkans
300+ excess deaths recorded in France during the heatwave
WMO (World Meteorological Organization) issued heatwave alerts
Event linked to climate change and intensification of extreme weather events

title: Venezuela Earthquake: WHO Warns Hospitals Near Breaking Point; 3-Year-Old Rescued After 6 Days
summary: Venezuela was struck by twin earthquakes, with rescuers continuing to dig for survivors nearly a week after the disaster. The World Health Organization (WHO) warned that Venezuelan hospitals are near breaking point due to the disaster. A 3-year-old child was rescued from a collapsed building six days after the quakes. The EU delivered emergency aid and organised humanitarian flights, while angry Venezuelans accused the government of negligence in the disaster response.
exam_angle: GS Paper 1 (Geography/Disaster Management) — earthquake-prone zones, seismic vulnerability, international humanitarian response mechanisms (WHO, EU), and disaster risk reduction. Venezuela is located in a seismically active Caribbean-Andean zone.
key_facts: Venezuela hit by twin earthquakes in late June 2026
WHO warned hospitals near breaking point post-earthquake
EU provided emergency aid and organised humanitarian flights
Venezuela is located in the Caribbean-Andean seismically active zone

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to Acquire Deutsche Bank's India Retail and Wealth Business for Rs 282 Crore
summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank announced the acquisition of Deutsche Bank's India retail banking and wealth management business for approximately Rs 282 crore (about $30 million). The deal marks Deutsche Bank's exit from the Indian retail banking segment. This consolidation reflects ongoing restructuring in India's private banking sector and Kotak's expansion strategy in retail and wealth management.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Economy) — bank mergers and acquisitions, foreign bank operations in India under RBI regulations, and financial sector consolidation. Relevant to understanding the evolving structure of India's banking industry.
key_facts: Acquirer: Kotak Mahindra Bank; Seller: Deutsche Bank (India retail and wealth business)
Deal value: ~Rs 282 crore (~$30 million)
Marks Deutsche Bank's exit from India's retail banking segment
Foreign banks in India operate under RBI's branch licensing norms

title: India-Japan Yen-Rupee Settlement Plan: Moving Away from Dollar in Bilateral Trade
summary: India and Japan are exploring a yen-rupee settlement mechanism for bilateral trade payments, which would reduce dependence on the US dollar as an intermediary currency. This move is part of India's broader push for currency internationalisation and aligns with global trends of de-dollarisation in trade. The plan is being discussed at the central bank and government levels and could benefit both nations by reducing exchange rate risks.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Economy) — currency internationalisation, de-dollarisation, India's Rupee Trade Settlement framework, and India's bilateral trade architecture. Connects to RBI's push for rupee-denominated trade settlements.
key_facts: India-Japan exploring yen-rupee direct settlement to bypass US dollar
Part of India's broader Rupee Trade Settlement and de-dollarisation push
RBI has already enabled rupee settlement with several countries (e.g., Russia, UAE)
Reduces transaction costs and exchange rate risk for bilateral traders

title: West Asia Remittances to India Rose 70% in April 2026 Despite Ongoing Conflict
summary: Net remittances to India from West Asia rose by 70% in April 2026 compared to the previous year, despite the ongoing conflict in the region, according to data reported by The Hindu. India is the world's largest recipient of remittances, with a significant share coming from the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries. The resilience of remittance flows underscores the continued employment of Indian workers in the Gulf.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Economy) — India's remittance economy, balance of payments, current account, and the role of the Indian diaspora in the Gulf. India's top remittance-receiving status is a frequently tested fact.
key_facts: Net remittances from West Asia to India rose 70% in April 2026
India is the world's largest recipient of remittances (World Bank data)
Gulf/West Asia accounts for a major share of India's total remittances
Remittances are a key component of India's current account credits

title: RBI Report: India's Economy Better Placed Than Most Peers Despite Global Risks
summary: An RBI report released on June 30, 2026 stated that India's economy is better positioned than most global peers in navigating current global risks, including geopolitical tensions, trade disruptions, and financial market volatility. The report highlighted India's strong macroeconomic fundamentals, robust domestic demand, and fiscal consolidation as key buffers. This assessment comes amid global uncertainties stemming from US tariff policies and Middle East conflicts.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Economy) — India's macroeconomic resilience, RBI's role as assessor of economic conditions, and India's positioning in the global economy. RBI reports (Annual Report, State of the Economy) are important sources for exam-relevant economic data.
key_facts: RBI report (June 2026): India's economy better placed than most peers
Strengths cited: strong domestic demand, fiscal consolidation, macroeconomic stability
Global risks include: US tariff actions, Middle East conflict, financial market volatility
RBI publishes monthly 'State of the Economy' bulletin and Annual Report

title: VB-G RAM G Act (Revamped MGNREGS Framework) to Come into Force from July 1, 2026
summary: Union Rural Development Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan announced that the VB-G RAM G Act will come into force from July 1, 2026, with the stated goal that no eligible rural worker should remain without work even for a single day. The Act appears to be a revamped legislative framework for rural employment guarantee, building upon the MGNREGS architecture. The Minister emphasised timely wage payments and universal coverage for eligible rural households.
exam_angle: GS Paper 2 (Governance/Social Justice) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) — rural employment guarantee schemes, MGNREGS reforms, and legislative frameworks for social protection. Direct employment guarantee legislation is a key GS topic.
key_facts: VB-G RAM G Act: new rural employment guarantee framework, effective July 1, 2026
Announced by: Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan (Rural Development)
Objective: ensure no eligible rural worker remains without work for even one day
Builds on MGNREGS (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme) architecture

title: Indian Startup Funding Falls 9% to $5.2 Billion in H1 2026
summary: Indian startup funding declined by 9% year-on-year to $5.2 billion in the first half of 2026 (H1 2026), according to data from Inc42. A separate report warned that restrictive regulations could slow Indian startup formation by 20% and venture capital (VC) flows by 25%. The funding slowdown reflects global risk-off sentiment, higher interest rates, and investor caution, even as India remains one of the world's top startup ecosystems.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Economy) — India's startup ecosystem, venture capital, DPIIT's role in startup policy (Startup India initiative), and the impact of regulatory environment on innovation and entrepreneurship.
key_facts: Indian startup funding: $5.2 billion in H1 2026, down 9% year-on-year (Inc42 data)
Restrictive regulations could reduce startup formation by 20% and VC flows by 25%
India ranks among the world's top 3 startup ecosystems by number of unicorns
DPIIT (Dept for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade) oversees Startup India policy

title: Delhi EV Policy 2026 Approved: Focus on Two-Wheeler Electrification
summary: The Delhi government cleared the Electric Vehicle (EV) Policy 2026, with a significant focus on electrifying two-wheelers, which constitute the largest segment of the city's vehicle fleet. The policy includes incentives for buyers, charging infrastructure targets, and scrapping of older petrol/diesel two-wheelers. Delhi's EV policy is aimed at reducing vehicular air pollution and meeting the city's clean air action plan commitments.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Environment/Economy) — urban air quality, EV transition policy, FAME scheme linkages, and state-level climate action. Delhi's persistent air pollution crisis and policy responses are a key exam topic.
key_facts: Delhi EV Policy 2026 cleared by Delhi government
Key focus: electrification of two-wheelers (largest vehicle category in Delhi)
Policy includes buyer incentives, charging infrastructure expansion, and old vehicle scrapping
Aims to reduce vehicular emissions contributing to Delhi's severe air pollution

title: Coal India to Invest Rs 1,900 Crore in R&D by FY2030, Pivots to Advanced Technologies
summary: Coal India Limited (CIL) announced plans to invest Rs 1,900 crore in research and development by FY2030, with a focus on advanced technologies including coal gasification, underground mining automation, and environmental mitigation. This investment signals CIL's strategic pivot towards cleaner coal technologies and operational efficiency as India transitions its energy mix. CIL is the world's largest coal mining company and a Navratna PSU.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy) — PSU investment in R&D, coal sector reforms, energy transition, and coal gasification as a clean coal technology. CIL's role in India's energy security and employment is exam-relevant.
key_facts: Coal India Limited (CIL): world's largest coal mining company; Navratna PSU
Planned R&D investment: Rs 1,900 crore by FY2030
Focus areas: coal gasification, underground mining automation, environmental technologies
CIL accounts for ~80% of India's total coal production

title: Cybersecurity Rules Proposed for Smart Vehicles in India to Prevent Malware Risks
summary: India's government proposed new cybersecurity rules for smart/connected vehicles to prevent malware attacks and unauthorised data access. As vehicles increasingly incorporate internet connectivity, over-the-air software updates, and AI-driven systems, cybersecurity has emerged as a critical safety concern. The proposed framework would mandate automakers to implement cybersecurity standards before vehicles are approved for sale in India.
exam_angle: GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Security) — cybersecurity regulations, connected vehicle technology, data protection, and India's emerging technology governance frameworks. Intersects with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
key_facts: India proposed mandatory cybersecurity standards for connected/smart vehicles
Risks include: malware attacks, remote hacking, unauthorised data access
Framework would require automaker compliance before type approval for sale
Connects to India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and IT Act frameworks
